symptoms_description: Order processor consumer group lag growing at 3000 messages/min on orders topic. 4 consumer instances running, 6 partitions. Lag started 22 minutes ago.
resolution_notes: Scaled order-processor from 4 to 6 replicas to match partition count. Lag cleared in 8 minutes.
summary: Consumer count below partition count caused throughput ceiling. Scaling consumers to partition count resolved growing lag.

symptoms_description: Payment processor stalled on partition 2 of payments topic. All other partitions healthy. Lag frozen at 847 messages for 15 minutes.
resolution_notes: Inspected offset 847234 on partition 2. Found malformed JSON payload with null amount field. Reset offset to 847235. Consumer resumed normally.
summary: Poison pill message with null amount field caused payment processor to deadlock. Offset skip resolved stall.

symptoms_description: All consumer groups lagging simultaneously. Lag spike of 85,000 messages across orders, payments, notifications within 3 minutes. Velocity declining.
resolution_notes: Identified as end-of-day batch job producing ~200k messages. Consumers caught up in 12 minutes without intervention. Producer throttling added as preventative measure.
summary: Scheduled batch job caused thundering herd. No consumer action needed — lag self-resolved as consumers processed the burst.

symptoms_description: Inventory updater consumer group completely stalled. Last offset commit was 25 minutes ago. Consumer pods show Running in Kubernetes.
resolution_notes: Thread dump revealed deadlock between DB connection acquisition and Kafka poll callback. Restarted pods. Added connection pool sizing fix.
summary: Database connection pool exhaustion caused consumer processing thread to block indefinitely. Pod restart cleared the deadlock.

symptoms_description: Orders lag growing at 8000 messages/min. Velocity accelerating. Consumer count equals partition count (6). CPU on consumer pods at 95%.
resolution_notes: Consumer count was already at partition count ceiling. Increased partitions from 6 to 12, scaled consumers to 12. Lag cleared in 20 minutes after rebalance.
summary: Consumer count at partition count ceiling prevented further horizontal scaling. Partition increase and consumer scale-out resolved critical growing lag.

symptoms_description: Partition 0 of user-events has 12,000 messages lag. Partitions 1-5 have 0 lag. Single consumer instance assigned to partition 0.
resolution_notes: Partition 0 stall traced to a single user event with a 4MB payload exceeding max.fetch.bytes. Skipped the message, added max size validation to producer.
summary: Oversized message (4MB) on partition 0 exceeded consumer fetch limits, stalling only that partition. Offset skip + producer validation resolved the issue.

symptoms_description: Payment processor lag growing steadily since new version deployment 35 minutes ago. Previous version had no lag. Lag at 45,000 messages.
resolution_notes: New deployment introduced N+1 DB query per message (missing cache initialization). DB connection pool saturated causing slow processing. Rolled back to previous version.
summary: Deployment regression introduced N+1 query pattern that slowed message processing 8x. Rollback resolved growing lag.

symptoms_description: Black Friday traffic spike. Orders topic receiving 15x normal volume. All partitions lagging but velocity slowing down. Consumers keeping partial pace.
resolution_notes: Pre-planned Black Friday scaling: doubled consumer replicas from 12 to 24. Lag peaked at 500k messages and cleared within 30 minutes.
summary: Expected traffic spike exceeded pre-provisioned consumer capacity. Pre-planned scale-out doubled throughput and lag cleared within SLA.

symptoms_description: All 3 notification consumer instances stopped committing offsets simultaneously. Pods show OOMKilled in Kubernetes events.
resolution_notes: OOMKilled caused by unbounded in-memory cache growing during high load. Increased pod memory limits and added LRU cache eviction. Restarted pods.
summary: Memory leak in consumer cache caused simultaneous OOMKilled across all instances. Increased memory limits and fixed cache eviction policy.

symptoms_description: Inventory lag growing at 500 messages/min. Redis cache returning high error rate, causing consumers to fall back to DB for every message.
resolution_notes: Redis connection pool was exhausted due to connection leaks. Restarted Redis, fixed connection leak in consumer code. Processing rate restored.
summary: Redis connection pool exhaustion caused consumers to bypass cache and hit DB for every message, reducing throughput 4x.

symptoms_description: Partitions 0, 1, 3, 4, 5 processing normally. Partition 2 lag growing for 20 minutes. Consumer assigned to partition 2 appears healthy.
resolution_notes: Consumer instance assigned to partition 2 had a stuck GC pause causing 45-second stop-the-world events. Restarted the specific pod resolved the stall.
summary: GC pause on a single consumer pod caused single partition stall. Pod restart cleared the GC issue.

symptoms_description: Orders consumer lag growing after infrastructure migration. Consumer count 6, partition count 6. Consumers appear to be processing but slowly.
resolution_notes: Migration reduced brokers from 3 to 2 accidentally. Broker 3 was taking partition leadership traffic causing network saturation on 2 brokers. Restored broker 3.
summary: Accidental broker removal during migration caused network saturation on remaining brokers, reducing consumer throughput 33%.

symptoms_description: User events lag spike to 30,000 messages during marketing campaign launch. Velocity already declining after 5 minutes.
resolution_notes: Marketing campaign caused 5x traffic spike lasting 8 minutes. Consumers caught up within 15 minutes. No intervention needed.
summary: Planned campaign traffic spike. Consumers self-recovered. No action taken.

symptoms_description: Payment processor completely stalled. All 6 consumers show healthy in Kubernetes. External payment API returning 429 (rate limited). Consumers retry-looping.
resolution_notes: Added exponential backoff to payment API calls. Without backoff, consumers were hammering the API causing infinite 429 loop. Added circuit breaker.
summary: Downstream API rate limiting caused consumers to enter infinite retry loop without backoff. Adding exponential backoff and circuit breaker resolved stall.

symptoms_description: Partition 1 lag at 340 messages, growing slowly. Other partitions at 0. Hot-spot key pattern detected on partition 1 (all messages from supplier ID 'SUP-001').
resolution_notes: SUP-001 sends inventory updates in large batches every hour. All updates hash to partition 1. Added salted partitioning for high-volume suppliers.
summary: Low-cardinality supplier key caused all updates from SUP-001 to land on partition 1. Salted partitioning distributed the load.

symptoms_description: Orders lag growing at 2000 msg/min. Consumer CPU at 30% — not CPU bound. DB query time at 800ms avg (normal is 20ms). DB under heavy load from another service.
resolution_notes: Identified noisy neighbor: analytics job running full table scans on orders DB. Cancelled the analytics job, DB latency returned to normal, consumer lag cleared.
summary: Noisy neighbor DB query caused consumer processing slowdown. Cancelling the interfering analytics query restored consumer throughput.

symptoms_description: Notifications lag growing at 400 msg/min. Email provider API latency increased from 200ms to 3000ms. Consumer throughput dropped proportionally.
resolution_notes: Email provider SLA breach. Scaled notification consumers from 3 to 9 to compensate for 15x latency increase. Lag stabilized. Provider restored service after 45 minutes.
summary: Email provider latency degradation caused throughput drop. Consumer scale-out compensated for the slowdown while provider recovered.

symptoms_description: User event tracker shows 0 active consumers in group. Consumer pods in CrashLoopBackOff after config map update.
resolution_notes: Config map update introduced invalid Kafka consumer config (typo in group.id). Fixed config map, pods recovered. Lag cleared in 5 minutes.
summary: Config map typo caused all consumer pods to crash on startup. Correcting the config restored the consumer group.

symptoms_description: Payments lag growing after certificate rotation. Consumers failing to connect to downstream fraud detection API (TLS handshake failure).
resolution_notes: Certificate rotation did not include the consumer application's trust store. Updated trust store with new CA certificate, restarted consumers.
summary: Certificate rotation caused TLS failures to fraud detection API. Consumer retries caused message processing stall. Trust store update resolved.

symptoms_description: Orders lag at 250,000 messages after 2-hour planned maintenance window. Consumers restarted and actively catching up at 12,000 msg/min. Velocity strongly negative.
resolution_notes: Post-maintenance catch-up in progress. No action needed. Lag cleared in 21 minutes.
summary: Post-maintenance catch-up successfully in progress. Consumers processing at 12,000 msg/min. No intervention needed.

symptoms_description: Inventory lag growing after Kafka broker 2 disk filled to 100%. Messages still produced but broker 2's partitions showing slow replication.
resolution_notes: Broker 2 disk at 100%. Deleted 7-day old log segments on broker 2. Freed 40GB. Replication and consumption returned to normal.
summary: Broker disk saturation caused replication lag and consumer throughput drop. Clearing old log segments freed disk and restored normal operation.

symptoms_description: Partition 0 of notifications stalled. Message at stuck offset contains a Slack notification with a 50KB message body — exceeding Slack API limits.
resolution_notes: Oversized Slack message body caused API rejection and infinite retry. Skipped the message, added payload size validation to notification producer.
summary: Oversized notification payload caused Slack API rejection and consumer retry loop. Offset skip and producer validation resolved.

symptoms_description: Orders lag spike to 180,000 messages. Identified as data migration script producing all historical orders into Kafka for reprocessing. Velocity already declining.
resolution_notes: Planned migration. Consumers caught up in 28 minutes. Migration team added throttling for future runs.
summary: Planned data migration produced burst load. Consumers self-recovered. Added producer throttling for future migration runs.

symptoms_description: Payment processor lag at 500,000 messages and growing. Kafka broker 1 is the leader for all 6 payment partitions after broker 2 and 3 crashes.
resolution_notes: Network switch failure isolated broker 2 and 3. Restored network connectivity. Brokers rejoined, ISR recovered, leader rebalancing distributed load across 3 brokers.
summary: Network switch failure caused 2 of 3 brokers to be isolated. Single broker handling all leader traffic caused throughput degradation. Network restoration resolved.

symptoms_description: User event tracker lag slowly growing since midnight. Consumer CPU at 15%, no obvious bottleneck. Investigation revealed consumer is discarding 60% of events due to schema validation failures.
resolution_notes: Schema registry version mismatch. Consumer loaded old schema that rejected new optional fields. Updated schema, restarted consumers. Discard rate dropped to 0%.
summary: Schema registry mismatch caused 60% message discard rate, reducing effective throughput. Schema update resolved the growing lag.

symptoms_description: Order processor stalled after Kubernetes node failure. 2 of 6 consumer pods are Pending due to insufficient cluster capacity. Remaining 4 pods handling all 12 partitions.
resolution_notes: Added new Kubernetes nodes to restore capacity. Pending pods became Running. 6 consumers processing 12 partitions (2 partitions each) — lag cleared in 10 minutes.
summary: Kubernetes node failure left consumers below partition count. Restoring node capacity brought consumers back online and lag cleared.

symptoms_description: Inventory lag growing at 1500 msg/min. Consumer max.poll.records was recently changed from 500 to 50, reducing batch efficiency 10x.
resolution_notes: max.poll.records change was intended for a different consumer group but applied to all. Reverted to 500 for inventory-updater. Lag cleared in 12 minutes.
summary: Accidental max.poll.records reduction from 500 to 50 reduced consumer throughput 10x. Reverting the config resolved growing lag.

symptoms_description: Partitions 0-4 healthy. Partition 5 lag at 22,000 and growing. Consumer 5 shows high GC pause times in JVM metrics.
resolution_notes: Consumer instance for partition 5 had accumulated excessive heap due to a large in-flight transactions list. GC couldn't keep up. Restart cleared heap.
summary: Memory accumulation caused GC thrashing on one consumer instance, stalling partition 5. Pod restart cleared the heap.

symptoms_description: Notifications lag growing after deploying new enrichment step that calls a product catalog API. API p99 latency at 2 seconds, causing consumer throughput to drop 5x.
resolution_notes: Scaled notification consumers to 9 while product catalog team investigated latency. Added local cache for catalog lookups. Lag cleared in 18 minutes.
summary: New API dependency introduced per-message latency overhead. Consumer scale-out compensated for slowdown while caching was implemented.

symptoms_description: Orders lag growing slowly at 200 msg/min over 2 hours. Consumer CPU normal. Gradual degradation traced to slow connection pool leak — connections not returned to pool.
resolution_notes: Connection pool leak causing gradual throughput degradation. Rolling restart of consumers reset connection pools. Fixed connection leak in application code.
summary: Slow DB connection pool leak caused gradual throughput degradation over 2 hours. Rolling restart and code fix resolved growing lag.

symptoms_description: End-of-quarter inventory reconciliation job produced 2 million messages in 10 minutes. All consumer groups lagging. No prior capacity planning.
resolution_notes: Emergency scale-out: inventory consumers 3→9, order consumers 12→24. Lag peak at 800k messages, cleared in 40 minutes.
summary: Unplanned quarterly batch job caused cluster-wide lag. Emergency consumer scale-out contained the damage. Capacity planning added for future quarter-end runs.

symptoms_description: User event tracker stalled. Consumer pods running but no offset commits. Investigation: consumer processing thread waiting on a distributed lock that was never released.
resolution_notes: Redis distributed lock TTL was set to 0 (infinite). Lock held by a crashed pod was never released. Added TTL of 60 seconds to all consumer locks. Released the stale lock manually.
summary: Distributed lock with infinite TTL was held by crashed pod. Remaining consumers blocked waiting for lock. Adding TTL and releasing stale lock resolved stall.

symptoms_description: Payments lag growing at 1200 msg/min. Idempotency check at DB was changed to use SELECT FOR UPDATE, causing lock contention between consumer threads.
resolution_notes: SELECT FOR UPDATE caused inter-thread contention reducing throughput 6x. Reverted to optimistic locking with unique constraint. Lag cleared in 15 minutes.
summary: Pessimistic locking change introduced DB lock contention between consumer threads. Reverting to optimistic locking restored throughput.

symptoms_description: Orders lag growing at 400 msg/min after log level changed to DEBUG in production. JSON serialization of debug objects was consuming 40% of processing time.
resolution_notes: Reverted log level to INFO. Throughput restored immediately. Debug logging serialization overhead was 40% of total processing time.
summary: Debug log level in production caused 40% CPU overhead from JSON serialization. Reverting to INFO log level immediately resolved growing lag.

symptoms_description: Partition 2 of inventory stalled. Message at offset contains a product with 10,000 image URLs in an array. Consumer processing time: 45 seconds per message.
resolution_notes: Data quality issue: product record had 10,000 image URLs instead of expected <100. Consumer fetching all images causing 45s processing time. Skipped and reported to data team.
summary: Data quality issue caused consumer to process a message with 10,000 image URLs, taking 45 seconds per message. Offset skip and data validation added.

symptoms_description: Notifications lag at 80,000 messages after SMS provider outage. Provider restored. Consumers catching up at 5000 msg/min. Estimated catch-up: 16 minutes.
resolution_notes: Consumers self-recovered after provider restoration. Lag cleared in 18 minutes. No action needed.
summary: Post-provider-outage catch-up successfully in progress. No intervention needed.

symptoms_description: Payments lag growing after Kafka broker upgrade from 3.2 to 3.5. Consumer protocol negotiation delay causing 2x fetch latency.
resolution_notes: Old consumer client version caused protocol negotiation overhead with new broker. Updated confluent-kafka to latest version. Fetch latency returned to normal.
summary: Consumer client version mismatch with upgraded broker caused fetch latency overhead. Client update resolved protocol negotiation delay.

symptoms_description: Order processor stalled. Consumers show REBALANCING state in consumer group describe. Rebalance has been running for 35 minutes without completing.
resolution_notes: Stuck rebalance caused by one consumer with session.timeout.ms=10000 while rebalance was taking 15 seconds. Increased session timeout to 60 seconds. Rebalance completed immediately.
summary: Session timeout shorter than rebalance duration caused infinite rebalance loop. Increasing session.timeout.ms resolved the stuck rebalance.

symptoms_description: User event lag growing. Identified hot partition pattern: 70% of messages going to partition 0 due to empty string user IDs being used as partition key.
resolution_notes: Mobile app bug: unauthenticated users sending empty string user_id. All empty keys hash to partition 0. Fixed app to use session ID as fallback key. Hot partition resolved.
summary: Mobile app bug caused null/empty user IDs to partition-hash to partition 0, creating extreme hot partition. App fix redistributed the load.

symptoms_description: Payment processor lag at 1.2M messages and growing. Root cause: Kafka compaction running on payments topic is blocking consumer fetch operations due to broker I/O saturation.
resolution_notes: Reduced log.cleaner.io.max.bytes.per.second from unlimited to 50MB/s to cap compaction I/O. Consumer fetch latency returned to normal within 5 minutes. Lag cleared in 35 minutes.
summary: Uncapped compaction I/O saturated broker disk bandwidth, increasing consumer fetch latency 10x. Throttling compaction I/O resolved critical growing lag.
